data.title: XTL Biopharmaceuticals announces 1-for-4 ADS ratio change
data.description:
• XTL Biopharmaceuticals (XTLB) will change its ADS ratio from 1:100 to 1:400 effective March 25, 2026.
  • The move is equivalent to a 1-for-4 reverse ADS split for shareholders.
  • No fractional ADSs will be issued; proceeds from fractional sales will be distributed in cash.

data.title: Earthwise Minerals plans private placement to raise up to $500,000
data.description:
• Earthwise Minerals (CSE:WISE)(FSE:966)(OTCQB:EWISF) on Friday said that it plans a non-brokered private placement of up to 10 million units to raise as much as $500,000.
  • The offering will include flow-through and non-flow-through units priced at $0.05 each.
  • Proceeds from the flow-through portion will fund exploration expenses, while the remainder will be used for working capital.
  • The company said the financing is subject to regulatory approvals and customary conditions.
  • EWISF closed -4.91% at $0.0348.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: U.S. drilling rig count drops for first time in three weeks, Baker Hughes says
data.description:
The number of oil and gas rigs actively drilling in the U.S. fell by 1 to 552, declining for the first time in three weeks, Baker Hughes said Friday in its latest survey.

During the week ended March 20, the number of active drilling rigs targeting crude oil in the U.S. rose by 2 to 414, the highest since mid-December, gas rigs fell by 2 to 131, and 7 rigs were classified as miscellaneous.

The total rig count was down by 41, or 6.9%, from the same time last year.

The number of rigs targeting oil in the Permian Basin increased by 2 to 243, the oil rig count in the Eagle Ford lost 1 to 32, and the Williston Basin added 3 to 29.

ETFs: (USO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: U.S. stocks end lower for a fourth consecutive week as Iran war concerns loom over sentiment
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages closed deep in the red on Friday, with oil sustaining higher prices even as Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu said the country is helping to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) ended -1.5%, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) finished -2%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) closed -1%.

Week-to-date, the indexes were -2%, -2.1%, and -2.1%, respectively.

All three indexes are near their correction territories. Meanwhile, the Russell 2000 (IWM) has officially entered a correction, falling 10.3% from its January 2022 closing high.

Over in the bond market, yields were sharply higher on Friday as traders reassessed the near-term path of monetary policy following the Federal Reserve’s latest decision to hold rates steady.

The benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was 14 basis points higher at 4.39%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose 10 basis points to 3.90%.

“Today was the largest options expiry in the history of options expiries and came on the heels of a decidedly alarmist week. All equity indices were red on the week,” said Seeking Alpha analyst Alex King of Cestrian Capital Research.

“Whether this reflects a reality that will settle in next week once re-hedging flows are settled, we have to wait until Monday and Tuesday to find out. The bulls are not down and out, as resilience in Bitcoin, Ether, and semiconductors (a higher low this week in SMH) evidence. We anticipate a liquidity-driven rally in the face of the fear,” King added.

Memory and AI-related stocks were lower, including shares of Nvidia (NVDA) -3.2%, Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) -1.9%, Broadcom (AVGO) -3%, and Qualcomm (QCOM) -1%.

Crude oil futures (CL1:COM) were higher at $97.9 on Friday, while Brent futures (CO1:COM) were at $112.1 per barrel.

In addition, the U.S. military is reportedly sending thousands more Marines to the Middle East, including three warships, according to U.S. officials on Friday. President Donald Trump also said the U.S. is aiming to inflict enough damage on Iran so that it can never rebuild its military.

data.title: Crypto bill sees development; Senators, White House reach tentative agreement - Politico
data.description:
The landmark crypto bill, which had been stalled in the Senate Banking Committee since January, could advance in the coming weeks. A tentative agreement was reached between Sen. Thom Tillis (R-N.C.), Sen. Angela Alsobrooks (D-Md.), and White House officials, according to Politico.

Key senators and the White House are said to have reached a tentative agreement on the language to be included in the cryptocurrency legislation, which aims to resolve differences between banks and digital asset firms over stablecoin yield.

The Clarity Act bill aims to create clear regulations that should help promote cryptocurrency adoption, say its supporters. However, the bill stalled in January after banks opposed a provision that would allow stablecoin issuers and crypto firms to offer yield-bearing products and other incentives that could draw deposits away from traditional banks, potentially making it harder for them to fund lending.

Crypto giants such as Coinbase (COIN) say they must be able to offer rewards to recruit ​customers, and barring them would be anticompetitive, while Standard Chartered (SCBFF) (SCBFY) has estimated that stablecoins could pull around $500 billion in deposits out of U.S. banks by ⁠the end of 2028.

What was included in the agreement was unclear and not guaranteed to win support from banking and crypto industries, the Friday, March 20, report noted.

"We've come a long way. And I think what it will do is to allow us to protect innovation, but also gives us the opportunity to prevent widespread deposit flight," Alsobrooks reportedly said in an interview.

"In working with the White House, I think we have an agreement," Tillis reportedly said. "Now we have to vet it with industry, because they are a party to an ultimate deal."

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: AEP, Japan's SoftBank strike public-private deal for giant Ohio gas-powered data center
data.description:
American Electric Power (AEP) announced Friday a partnership with Japan's SoftBank (SFTBY) (SFTBF) to build a massive AI data center on federally owned land in Ohio, part of a wider trade deal between the U.S. and Japan to include multi-billion-dollar investments in advanced digital and energy infrastructure.

The investments, when approved, will include new 765 kV electric transmission infrastructure to serve data center development at the site of the former gaseous diffusion plant in Piketon, Ohio, where a 10 GW data center campus is planned for the site.

Softbank (SFTBY) (SFTBF) is committed to paying for the $4.2B in new transmission investments to help avoid increases to transmission rates for Ohio residents; AEP (AEP) said it expects power to begin flowing to the site in 2029.

The public-private partnership includes $33.3B in funding from Japan for 9.2 GW of new natural gas generation, the ​U.S. Department of Energy said.

AEP (AEP) said it is preparing to engage with community members and landowners to solicit feedback; the Ohio Power Siting Board will oversee the permitting of any new transmission lines.

data.title: Georgia opens the gas-tax playbook on oil shock
data.description:
The state of Georgia on Friday responded to the global energy shock with a fuel tax holiday, offering an early look at how U.S. policymakers may try to blunt the hit from rising oil and natural gas prices. 

Governor Brian Kemp signed HB 1199 into law, triggering a 60-day suspension of the state levy on gasoline and diesel, according to a release. Georgia's excise tax is 33.3 cents on gasoline and 37.3 cents on diesel, and the governor's office said pump prices should start reflecting the change in the coming days.

The move gives the market an early glimpse of how policymakers may try to lean against the inflationary hit from higher energy (CO1:COM) (CL1:COM) (NG1:COM) prices induced by the war in Iran. As RSM Chief Economist Joseph Brusuelas put it in a social media post, "this is the first of what will be many attempts by the policy sector to mitigate the oil shock."

"Today's suspension of the state gas tax enabled by House Bill 1199 will save hardworking Georgians and the truckers who keep our state moving nearly $400 million over the next 60 days," said Speaker Jon Burns.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Nasdaq sinks 2% to a new 6-month trading low
data.description:
The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) extended its losses Friday afternoon, falling 2% as weakness across major technology names continued to weigh on the index. The decline has pushed the benchmark to its lowest level in more than six months, revisiting levels not seen since early September.

Market breadth within the Nasdaq-100 (NDX) remains notably weak, with 81 of its 100 constituents trading lower on the session, underscoring broad-based selling pressure rather than isolated declines. The downturn reflects continued risk aversion among investors, particularly in high-growth and rate-sensitive sectors. 

Outlined below are the 10 worst-performing NDX members in Friday’s trading session: 

  1. Constellation Energy Corporation (CEG), -9.3%
  2. Western Digital Corporation (WDC), -6.8%
  3. CrowdStrike Holdings (CRWD), -5.4%
  4. Micron Technology (MU), -5.3%
  5. ASML Holding (ASML), -5.2%
  6. Intel Corporation (INTC), -5.1%
  7. Shopify (SHOP), -5.1%
  8. Seagate Technology Holdings (STX), -4.8%
  9. Lam Research Corporation (LRCX), -4.3%
  10. Insmed Incorporated (INSM), -4.1% 

Nasdaq ETFs: (QQQ), (QQQM), (SQQQ), (TQQQ), (QLD), and (QID).

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Eyes on post-Iran economic data, Fedspeak, and cybersecurity
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - March 23

  • Volatility watch - Coinbase (COIN) and Lululemon (LULU) have a high level of implied volatility based on options trading. Sunrun (RUN) and Omega Therapeutics (OMGAQ) head into the new week with a high level of short interest outstanding on them. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Tilly’s (TLYS), Adecoagro (AGRO), and Kore Group (KORE). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Crane Harbor Acquisition (CHAC), Lifecore Biomedical (LFCR), and HNI (HNI).
  • Energy watch - Oil prices are likely to remain volatile next week, with levels heavily dependent on Middle East headlines, shipping developments, and supply reports.
  • Fedspeak - Federal Reserve speakers next week include Vice Chair for Supervision Michael Barr, Governor Adriana Kugler, Governor Philip Jefferson, San Francisco Fed President Mary Daly, and Philadelphia Fed President Anna Paulson, along with additional remarks by Governor Stephen Miran.
  • Dividend watch - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Broadcom (AVGO), Altria (MO), Seagate (STX), and Dick’s Sporting Goods (DKS).
  • IPO watch - No new IPOs are expected to price during the week and begin trading. The IPO lockup period expires for certain blocks of shares of Megan Holdings (MGN). The analyst quiet period ends on Generate Biomedicines (GENB) to free up analysts to post ratings.
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include WeRide (WRD).
  • All day - Climb Global Solutions (CLMB) will begin trading on a split-adjusted basis following the four-for-one stock split.

 

Tuesday - March 24

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include GameStop (GME), Smithfield Foods (SFD), and Braze (BRZE).
  • All day - Shareholders with Two Harbors Investment (TWO) will vote on the deal to be acquired by mortgage wholesaler UWM Holdings (UWMC).
  • All day - JFB Construction (JFB) will begin trading on a split-adjusted basis following the two-for-one stock split.
  • All day - Flash PMIs for economies around the world will give investors a chance to assess the economic impact of the Middle East conflict.
  • All day - The ShopTalk conference in Las Vegas will include a keynote from Reddit (RDDT) CEO Steve Huffman. Top execs from Wayfair (W), Stitch Fix (SFIX), Pinterest (PINS), Gap (GAP), Dutch Bros (BROS), and SharkNinja (SN) will also appear.
  • All day - MercadoLibre (MELI) will appear at a Morgan Stanley conference.
  • 9:00 a.m. Zillow Group (ZG) will host an investor event focused on how the company continues to deliver and lead on AI innovation in real estate.
  • 10:00 a.m. The New Home Sales report will be released by the Census Bureau.
  • 1:50 p.m. CrowdStrike (CRWD) CEO George Kurtz will give a keynote address at the RSA security conference.
  • 2:00 p.m. Asure Software's (ASUR) CEO will participate in a panel discussion on AI and Implications for Business Services and Software at the Roth Conference.
  • 4:30 p.m. The Nasdaq short interest report will be released.

 

Wednesday - March 25

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include PDD (PDD), Paychex (PAYX), Chewy (CHWY), Winnebago (WGO), and Jefferies (JEF). Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for Noodles (NDLS) and Widepoint (WYY) after their reports.
  • All day - Dana (DAN) will hold a Capital Markets Day event in New York City centered on its long‑term "Dana 2030" value‑creation strategy, targeting roughly $10B of annual sales by 2030, margin expansion, and improved free cash flow.
  • All day - Generac Holdings (GNRC) will hold its Investor Day event.
  • All day - Starbucks (SBUX) will hold its annual meeting. One of the items up for a vote is a proposal for an independent board chair by separating the roles of chair and CEO and adopting an independent chair policy.
  • 2:00 p.m. Varonis (VRNS) CEO Yaki Faitelson will give a keynote address at the RSA security conference.

 

Thursday - March 26

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Commercial Metals Company (CMC). 
  • M&A votes - Shareholders with Heritage Commerce (HTBK) and CVB Financial (CVBF) vote on their all-stock deal valued at about $811M. Shareholders with ON24 (ONTF) will vote on the buyout deal from Cvent.
  • All day - The Federal Reserve's Senior Loan Officer Opinion Survey on Bank Lending Practices will be released. The SLOOS report will provide an update on the conditions, supply, and demand of bank loans extended to customers in the U.S. Tightening standards can signal stress in the banking system or broader economy, impacting financial stocks.
  • All day - G7 foreign ministers will meet to discuss issues including the Iran war.
  • 9:00 a.m. Marex Group (MRX) will hold its Investor Day event.
  • 2:00 p.m. Genius Sports Limited (GENI) will host a strategic NewFront event as part of the official IAB NewFronts week. The NewFront will also feature conversations with leaders from Publicis Sports, NBCUniversal (CMCSA), the NFL and WNBA, Lowe's (LOW), and others, exploring how live sports is evolving into a real-time, data-powered, and addressable media environment.

 

Friday - March 27

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Carnival (CCL).

 

Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this story.

data.title: Air Products upgraded at J.P. Morgan on helium price recovery
data.description:
Air Products and Chemicals (APD) down 1.1% in Friday's trading despite receiving an upgrade from J.P. Morgan to Overweight from Neutral with a $310 price target, raised from $280, citing the reversal in helium prices due to the closing of the Strait of Hormuz and attacks on the Qatari Ras Laffan energy complex, which is responsible for ~30% of global helium production.

J.P. Morgan's Jeffrey Zekauskas said he expects Air Products (APD) to be a better relative share price performer in an economic environment where the risks of economic deceleration, higher inflation, and upward interest rate movements have increased because of the stability of its earnings growth, adding that half of the company's revenue comes from onsite operations where higher energy costs are passed through to customers.

Air Products (APD) likely will see improved utilization rates among its chemicals and refinery customers in North America given their relative cost advantages, the analyst said; 20%-25% of the company's total revenues stem from chemicals end markets, 20%-25% from refining, and 15%-20% from electronics.
